title: Three-phase interactive loop, --check flag, exit-code semantics, and config/allowed_roots guided fixes
action: Implement D14's three-phase structure and wire the first guided fixes. Phase 1 (diagnose): build_checks() in checks.rs must never early-return. The socket-resolution failure at checks.rs:96-107 (the 'herdr endpoint' check, the return checks; is at line 105) stays a Check::fail entry but no longer aborts. Add a new field to the Check struct (checks.rs:9-15) to distinguish 'skipped because an earlier check failed' from a real pass/fail/info - e.g. `pub skipped: bool` (default false via the existing ok/fail/info constructors, true via a new Check::skipped(label, reason) constructor). Update print_report (mod.rs:20-28) to render skipped checks with their own marker, and the exit-code predicate (mod.rs:14, `checks.iter().all(|c| c.ok || !c.critical)`) so a skipped check is never treated as a pass on its own critical flag - decide and document whether skipped counts as blocking; the safest default is non-blocking (matching today's behavior where these checks simply never ran and did not block exit 0 by themselves). Only the two checks that directly consume the `socket` value produced right before the early return - check 4 (socket file exists, uses socket.exists() at checks.rs:110) and check 5 (herdr reachable, uses SocketHerdr::new(socket) at checks.rs:122) - become skipped when the socket resolution failed; do not change any other check's logic, and do not assume any fixed total check count (build_checks() already omits checks 7/9 when config is None and check 10 when no dev service is active - checks.rs:156,157-171,193-205,207-209; that variable count is unchanged, this cell only removes the abort). Phase 2 (offer fix, interactive only, gated by prompt::is_interactive()): for exactly two check IDENTITIES (matched by label, not by ok/fail state, since a fixable problem can present as either): (a) the 'config' check - when it is Check::info (missing, checks.rs:84-87, ok:true) offer to confirm+ensure_config() (existing helper, unchanged); when it is Check::fail (present but invalid, checks.rs:71-77, ok:false) use write::diagnose_fields on the raw file text to find invalid fields, prompt for a replacement value per invalid field (prompt::prompt_line), then write::repair_fields and write::persist_validated - if diagnose_fields returns RepairOutcome::Unparseable, confirm then write::backup_and_recreate per D7; (b) the 'allowed roots' check - it can present as Check::fail 'N configured root(s) are missing' (checks.rs:157-169, a missing directory, not literally empty) OR the roots list can be entirely absent because the 'config' check's own validation already failed the whole config load with ConfigError::EmptyAllowedRoots (an empty allowed_roots never reaches check 7 at all, since config is None in that case) - handle both: when 'allowed roots' fails, offer to fix the missing directories or add one; when 'config' fails specifically because the raw JSON's allowed_roots is empty, offer to add one there as part of the config-repair flow. Adding any new root path: prompt::prompt_line for the path, then classify it with write::classify_root_breadth(candidate, home) - resolve `home` via the existing native per-user profile logic (config::home() at src/config/mod.rs:735 is private; either make it `pub(crate)`/`pub` so this cell can call it, or add a small pub wrapper in config/mod.rs - do not reimplement platform home-directory resolution). When classify_root_breadth returns anything other than RootBreadth::Narrow (FilesystemRoot/HomeDirectory/Symlink), require prompt::typed_confirm with an explicit warning naming the breadth kind before accepting it, per D9 - Narrow needs no extra confirmation, and there is no path that skips this classification call. Every fix path uses write::persist_validated (or backup_and_recreate) so an invalid candidate is never actually written, per D6. Phase 3 (recheck): in INTERACTIVE mode only, after phase 2 (whether or not any fix was applied - the re-run is unconditional whenever phase 2 ran, per D11), call build_checks() again exactly once for a final report; do not re-run per-check. In --check mode or non-interactive mode (piped/redirected stdin or stderr, prompt::is_interactive() false), phase 2 and phase 3 never run at all - the function returns after phase 1's single build_checks() call and single print_report(), byte-identical to today's report and exit code, with --check taking precedence over TTY detection per D15. main.rs's doctor dispatch (currently `let ok = herdr_go::doctor::run().await; std::process::exit(if ok {0} else {1});`) changes so run()'s bool reflects whichever phase actually ran last (phase 1 alone in --check/non-interactive; phase 3's recheck in interactive mode) - never the interactive phase-1 pre-fix state when phase 2/3 did run. Add a `--check` boolean flag to Args and the arg-parsing match arm (D15).

truths: build_checks() never early-returns; every check that does not depend on a failed socket resolution still runs and is reported, in the same conditional-presence pattern as today (checks 7/9/10 remain conditionally absent exactly as they are now - this cell does not change which checks can be omitted, only removes the abort).
Only check 4 (socket exists) and check 5 (herdr reachable) become skipped when socket resolution fails; every other check's logic, presence conditions, and text are unchanged.
A skipped check is visually and structurally distinct from ok/fail/info in both the Check struct and print_report's rendering, and does not count as a pass toward the exit-code predicate on its own.

Interactive mode offers a fix for the 'config' check identity in both its failure-shaped states (missing = info, invalid = fail) and for the 'allowed roots' problem in both its entry points (the check-7 missing-directory failure, and an empty-allowed_roots config-load failure surfaced through the 'config' check) - every other check's failure still only prints advice, exactly as today.

A path classified as FilesystemRoot, HomeDirectory, or Symlink by write::classify_root_breadth is never added to allowed_roots without an explicit typed confirmation naming the breadth kind; a Narrow path needs no extra confirmation; classify_root_breadth's home resolution reuses the existing native per-user profile logic, never a reimplementation.
An invalid candidate config is never persisted - every write path goes through write::persist_validated or write::backup_and_recreate, never a raw file write.
In interactive mode, after phase 2 (run or not), phase 3 always re-runs all checks exactly once, and the process exit code reflects this final post-fix state.

--check produces byte-identical output and exit-code behavior to today's doctor report, regardless of TTY status, never prompts, never writes, and never triggers a phase-3 re-run (there is nothing to re-run after).

Non-interactive invocation without --check (piped/redirected stdin or stderr) also stays exactly phase-1-only, byte-identical to today's report - D5's existing parity is preserved, not just --check's.
migrate_default_state_if is still never invoked anywhere on the doctor code path, per D12.

artifacts.substantive: Check struct gains a skipped discriminant, early return removed, config and allowed_roots checks wired to their guided fixes matched by check identity across both their failure-shaped states

three-phase run() orchestration: diagnose, offer-fix (interactive only), single unconditional recheck (interactive only), print_report renders the skipped state, exit-code predicate treats skipped as non-blocking, exit-code-driving bool reflects whichever phase actually ran last
--check flag added to Args and arg parsing, precedence over TTY detection

key_links: checks.rs's config/allowed_roots fixes call src/config/write.rs's persist_validated/repair_fields/diagnose_fields/backup_and_recreate/classify_root_breadth - never hand-rolled validation or a raw fs::write
mod.rs's interactive gate reuses src/doctor/prompt.rs's is_interactive/prompt_line/typed_confirm - never a new TTY check
classify_root_breadth's home argument comes from src/config/mod.rs's existing native-profile home resolution (currently private fn home() at mod.rs:735) exposed for this call, not reimplemented
prohibitions: Do not wire a guided fix for any check other than config and allowed_roots in this cell - the remaining fixable checks (web/github/telegram secrets) are the next cell's scope.
Do not add the settings editor or its end-of-run prompt in this cell - that is the next cell's scope.
Do not add a --yes or any blanket auto-apply flag, per D15.
Do not re-run individual checks after a fix - only one full re-run of all checks after the entire fix phase, per D14, and only in interactive mode.
Do not change any check's diagnostic logic, text, or presence conditions beyond removing the early return and adding the skipped state to checks 4 and 5.
Do not weaken the existing non-TTY/piped-stdout read-only behavior, and do not run phase 3 in --check or non-interactive mode.
Do not gate the config/allowed_roots guided fixes on a bare !check.ok test - match by check identity across both failure-shaped states as specified.

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash tests/rename_contract.sh

trace.outcome: Implemented D14 three-phase interactive doctor (diagnose/offer-fix/recheck), --check flag with TTY precedence, skipped-check discriminant with non-blocking exit semantics, and guided fixes for config + allowed_roots matched by check identity. Breadth guard (D9) factored into reusable prompt_new_allowed_root. Exposed config::home() pub(crate).

checks.evidence: checks.rs: return checks; removed, socket resolution now yields Some(socket)/None; checks 7/9/10 conditional blocks untouched by the diff
checks.rs:153 and :186 - only 'herdr endpoint' (check 4) and 'herdr reachable' (check 5) gain a None => Check::skipped(...) arm; no other check's Ok/Fail/text changed

checks.rs:20 pub skipped: bool field + Check::skipped ctor; mod.rs render_report marks with a distinct symbol and separate summary line; all_ok uses c.ok || c.skipped || !c.critical - skipped exempted by its own clause, ok stays false

offer_fixes matches by check.label, not !ok; offer_config_fix re-derives state from file (missing->ensure_config, invalid->diagnose_fields); empty allowed_roots handled in apply_field_repairs; offer_allowed_roots_fix handles missing-dir failure

prompt_new_allowed_root: Narrow->accept directly; FilesystemRoot/HomeDirectory/Symlink->typed_confirm with kind-naming warning; home passed from crate::config::home(), config/mod.rs:735 exposed pub(crate) not reimplemented
persist_and_report -> write::persist_validated; unparseable -> write::backup_and_recreate; missing -> config::ensure_config (existing default writer); no raw fs::write of config
mod.rs run(): after offer_fixes block, single unconditional final_checks = build_checks().await; print_report; all_ok(&final_checks)
mod.rs:23 if check_only || !prompt::is_interactive() { return all_ok(&checks); } short-circuits before phase-2/3, check_only tested first
same guard; is_interactive() requires both stdin AND stderr terminals; report-content difference is the accepted D14/D5 change, no prompt/write/phase-3 leak
grep migrate_default_state_if src/doctor/ returns nothing; main.rs migrate call unchanged, its own guard excludes doctor
main.rs:22 check: bool on Args; parse arm --check => check = true; run(args.check); precedence via check_only-first ordering
